# memorybank

Persistent cross-session memory for AI agents.

## What it does
memorybank is an MCP server and CLI tool that gives AI coding agents persistent memory across sessions. When a session ends, memories are saved as local markdown files. When a new session starts, the agent can recall relevant context.

## Install
npx @ura-dev/memorybank --help

## MCP Setup
Add to MCP config:
{"mcpServers":{"memorybank":{"command":"npx","args":["-y","@ura-dev/memorybank","memorybank-mcp"]}}}

## Tools
- memory_store: Save a new memory with type (user/project/feedback/reference/custom), name, content, optional tags
- memory_recall: Retrieve most relevant memories for a query, returns full content
- memory_list: List all memories, filter by type or tag
- memory_get: Get a specific memory by its 12-char hex ID
- memory_update: Update an existing memory (partial update)
- memory_delete: Delete a memory by ID
- memory_search: Full-text search across all memories with relevance scoring
- memory_stats: Get counts by type, total storage size

## Storage
File-based: ~/.memorybank/<namespace>/memories/<id>.md
Each memory is a markdown file with YAML frontmatter (id, name, type, description, created, updated, tags).
Index maintained at ~/.memorybank/<namespace>/index.json.
Namespaces isolate memories per project.

## Requirements
Node.js >= 18. Zero dependencies.

## Links
npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@ura-dev/memorybank
GitHub: https://github.com/ura-tools/memorybank
License: MIT
